$comment: keemakr Marketplace submission for the kee-hello agent. This is the EntrySubmission the platform's registerEntry() ingests (see keemakr-core src/lib/marketplace/types.ts). Installing it for a tenant creates the 'hello' department + its single agent, gated on the one required connection (Hunter.io) below.
description: A tiny demo agent: greets and shouts text, and finds work emails via Hunter.io. The smallest Marketplace entry that uses a real tenant connection end to end.
author: keemakr

department.label: Hello
department.description: A one-agent demo department that greets and shouts text.

agents.role: Greets and shouts text; finds work emails
agents.description: Greets and shouts text (shout), and finds a person's work email at a company domain via Hunter.io (find-email). No database, no subagents.

dependencies.reason: Used by the find-email tool to look up work emails. The tenant connects their own Hunter.io key; the agent calls it through keemakr's capability proxy and never sees the key.

changelog: Add find-email (Hunter.io) — the agent now uses its declared connection end to end via @keemakr/tools + @keemakr/agent-sdk.
